# Lane Audit: Hive

## Lane Summary

- Audit status: `manual-pass-complete`
- Section start commit: `Add Hive environments`
- Section end commit: `Remove legacy SuiteHiveTransactional`
- Introduced JUnit suites: `SuiteAuthorization`, `SuiteHdfsImpersonation`, `SuiteHiveAlluxioCaching`, `SuiteHiveBasic`,
  `SuiteHiveKerberos`, `SuiteHiveSpark`, `SuiteHiveStorageFormats`, `SuiteHiveTransactional`, `SuiteHmsOnly`,
  `SuiteStorageFormatsDetailed`, `SuiteTwoHives`.
- Extended existing suites: none.
- Retired legacy suites: `SuiteHmsOnly`, `SuiteStorageFormatsDetailed`, `SuiteHiveTransactional`.
- Environment classes introduced: `DeltaLakeMinioEnvironment`, `DeltaLakeOssEnvironment`, `HiveBasicEnvironment`,
  `HiveHudiRedirectionsEnvironment`, `HiveIcebergRedirectionsEnvironment`, `HiveImpersonationEnvironment`,
  `HiveKerberosEnvironment`, `HiveSparkEnvironment`, `HiveSparkNoStatsFallbackEnvironment`,
  `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`, `HiveTransactionalEnvironment`, `HudiEnvironment`, `MultinodeHive4Environment`,
  `MultinodeHiveCachingEnvironment`, `TwoKerberosHivesEnvironment`, `TwoMixedHivesEnvironment`.
- Method status counts: verified `547`, intentional difference `110`, needs follow-up `0`.

## Semantic Audit Status

- Manual review note: the remaining Hive pass was completed by re-reading the previously unresolved legacy/current
  method bodies, the remaining kerberized and Spark no-stats environment source, and the current suite wiring against
  the already recorded method-by-method baseline below. The previously recorded Hive gaps were then restored in code
  and revalidated against the updated source.

- Method semantic audit: `complete`
- Environment semantic audit: `complete`
- Suite semantic audit: `complete`

## Lane-Level Open Questions / Intentional Differences

- Lane-wide approved difference set: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`.
- Needs-follow-up methods: none.

## Environment Semantic Audit
### `HiveKerberosImpersonationEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: manual comparison of legacy `EnvSinglenodeKerberosHiveImpersonation` against current
  `HiveKerberosImpersonationEnvironment`.
- Container/service inventory parity: preserved in intent. Both provide kerberized Hadoop/Hive services plus Trino with
  Hive authorization and impersonation coverage on the same lane.
- Config/resource wiring parity: preserved in intent. Legacy launcher mounted prepared Hive and Iceberg connector files;
  current environment programs the same Kerberos, sql-standard security, and impersonation settings directly in code and
  executes Hive statements through beeline-backed helpers.
- Startup/auth parity: preserved in intent. Current default Trino connections pre-set `SET ROLE admin IN hive` to
  reproduce the legacy shared-session admin-role behavior used by the authorization tests.
- Recorded differences: `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`, outside-Docker execution,
  JUnit/Testcontainers framework replacement, and code-driven configuration instead of launcher-time file mounting.
- Reviewer note: no additional environment-specific fidelity gap is currently identified.

### `HiveImpersonationEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against the non-kerberos impersonation launcher path in legacy `Suite2` and current `HiveImpersonationEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy impersonation coverage ran inside the shared launcher `EnvSinglenodeHdfsImpersonation`; current environment extracts that shape into a dedicated Testcontainers class.
  - Current environment builds the Hive connector properties directly instead of copying launcher-side `hive.properties`, but preserves the two key impersonation settings: HDFS impersonation and metastore thrift impersonation.
  - Current environment narrows the runtime surface to the single Trino container plus Hadoop instead of the broader launcher standard cluster.
- Reviewer note: the current environment is a direct extraction of the legacy non-kerberos impersonation lane and preserves the impersonation-specific semantics.

### `HiveKerberosImpersonationCredentialCacheEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: manual comparison of legacy `EnvSinglenodeKerberosHiveImpersonationWithCredentialCache`
  against current `HiveKerberosImpersonationCredentialCacheEnvironment`.
- Authentication parity: preserved in intent. Both swap the Trino Kerberos keytab path for a generated credential-cache
  location while keeping the same Hive impersonation and SQL-standard authorization lane.
- Config/resource wiring parity: preserved in intent. Legacy launcher mounted credential-cache-oriented connector files;
  current environment derives from `HiveKerberosImpersonationEnvironment` and overrides only the credential-cache
  generation and catalog authentication properties.
- Recorded differences: `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`, outside-Docker execution,
  JUnit/Testcontainers framework replacement, and code-driven configuration instead of launcher-time file mounting.
- Reviewer note: no additional environment-specific fidelity gap is currently identified.

### `MultinodeHiveCachingEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against legacy launcher `EnvMultinodeHiveCaching` and current `MultinodeHiveCachingEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy environment layered caching onto `StandardMultinode` plus shared Hadoop; current environment constructs an explicit two-node Trino cluster and Hadoop container directly.
  - Legacy coordinator/worker tmpfs mounts were attached through launcher-wide container customization; current environment applies the same cache mount at cluster construction time.
  - Current environment creates both cached and non-cached Hive catalogs directly instead of mounting two static properties files.
- Reviewer note: the caching-vs-noncached topology is preserved, and the current environment remains semantically faithful to the legacy Alluxio-caching lane.

### `HiveBasicEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against the `EnvMultinode`/`HDFS_NO_IMPERSONATION` launcher path in legacy `Suite2` and current `HiveBasicEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy coverage ran on the shared launcher multinode environment; current environment extracts the relevant Hive/HDFS shape into a single Trino container plus Hadoop container.
  - Current environment creates the TPCH-backed `nation`, `region`, and `orders` Hive tables during startup instead of inheriting them from launcher bootstrap scripts.
  - Current environment exposes several dedicated Hive catalogs directly (`hive_with_external_writes`, `hive_timestamp_nanos`, `hive_with_run_view_as_invoker`) rather than through mounted static files.
- Reviewer note: the environment is narrower than the old launcher topology but preserves the non-impersonation Hive semantics used by the current `SuiteHiveBasic` tests.

### `HiveKerberosEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against the kerberized launcher paths in legacy `Suite2` and current `HiveKerberosEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy kerberized Hive coverage relied on the launcher’s `HadoopKerberos` component layered onto shared standard containers; current environment brings the KDC, Hadoop, and Trino service wiring into one dedicated Testcontainers class.
  - Current environment writes keytabs and `krb5.conf` into a temporary host directory and bind-mounts them into Hadoop/Trino rather than relying on launcher init helpers.
  - Kerberos deployment cleanup is visible in the current environment structure, but the intended principals, keytabs, and Hive/Trino Kerberos semantics remain aligned with legacy intent.
- Reviewer note: this is an approved Kerberos deployment cleanup without an intended coverage change.

### `HiveSparkEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against the legacy Hive/Spark compatibility launcher environment and current `HiveSparkEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Current environment extracts Spark/Hive compatibility into a dedicated class instead of layering Spark services onto the generic launcher stack.
  - The Hive side now targets the approved Apache Hive 3.1 stack instead of the legacy HDP stack.
  - Current environment provisions the catalogs and Spark-facing resources directly in code instead of through launcher-provisioned config directories.
- Reviewer note: the environment remains within the approved `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration` envelope and preserves the Spark/Hive interoperability intent.

### `HiveSparkNoStatsFallbackEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: manual comparison of legacy `EnvSinglenodeSparkHiveNoStatsFallback` against current
  `HiveSparkNoStatsFallbackEnvironment`.
- Container/service inventory parity: preserved in intent. Both run Hadoop/Hive metastore, Spark Thrift Server, and
  Trino with the no-stats-fallback Hive configuration for the Spark compatibility lane.
- Config/resource wiring parity: preserved in intent. The current environment sets
  `hive.metastore.thrift.use-spark-table-statistics-fallback=false` directly in the Trino catalog and builds the Spark
  service in code instead of mounting launcher-side `hive.properties` and `spark-defaults.conf`.
- Recorded differences: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`, outside-Docker execution, JUnit/Testcontainers framework
  replacement, and code-driven configuration instead of launcher-time file mounting.
- Reviewer note: no additional environment-specific fidelity gap is currently identified.

### `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against the broad storage-format launcher paths from legacy `Suite2`, `SuiteHmsOnly`, and `SuiteStorageFormatsDetailed`, and current `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy storage-format coverage ran on multiple launcher environments (`EnvMultinode`, kerberized single-node variants, and credential-cache variants); current environment isolates the non-kerberized storage-format baseline into one dedicated class.
  - Current environment directly manages Hive JDBC sessions, HDFS helpers, and multiple Hive catalogs for timestamp precision, external writes, and view behavior instead of relying on launcher-side shared setup.
  - The current environment is narrower than the full legacy launcher spread; kerberized and impersonation storage-format coverage is now routed through separate suites/environments.
- Reviewer note: the extraction is semantically faithful for the non-kerberized storage-format lane, while the broader kerberized coverage is intentionally represented elsewhere in the Hive tranche.

### `HiveTransactionalEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against legacy launcher `EnvSinglenodeHiveAcid` and current `HiveTransactionalEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy transactional coverage used a dedicated single-node ACID launcher image; current environment recreates the transactional Hive/HDFS shape directly with explicit container startup and catalog properties.
  - The current environment operates on Apache Hive 3.1 rather than the legacy HDP distribution, which is an approved lane-wide difference.
  - Current transactional helpers are exposed as direct environment methods instead of through launcher bootstrap and Tempto executors.
- Reviewer note: the dedicated transactional lane remains faithful to the old ACID test intent within the approved Hive 3.1 migration envelope.

### `TwoKerberosHivesEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against legacy launcher `EnvTwoKerberosHives` and current `TwoKerberosHivesEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy environment used launcher `HadoopKerberos` plus a second kerberized Hadoop image with copied keytabs; current environment starts the KDC, both Hadoop clusters, and Trino directly inside one Testcontainers class.
  - Current environment continues to present paired Hive and Iceberg catalogs for both clusters, but generates and mounts the keytabs itself instead of relying on launcher resource providers.
  - Kerberos deployment cleanup is visible in the current implementation, but the two-kerberos-cluster semantics remain intact.
- Reviewer note: this is an approved Kerberos deployment cleanup without an intended change to the two-Hives coverage.

### `TwoMixedHivesEnvironment`
- Environment semantic audit status: `complete`
- Legacy/current basis: compared against legacy launcher `EnvTwoMixedHives` and current `TwoMixedHivesEnvironment`.
- Recorded differences:
  - Legacy environment combined launcher `HadoopKerberos` with a second non-kerberized Hadoop image; current environment starts both clusters directly and wires the mixed-auth catalogs in code.
  - Current environment generates the kerberized cluster keytabs and init script itself instead of relying on launcher common components and copied resources.
  - The mixed pair of kerberized and non-kerberized Hive and Iceberg catalogs is preserved.
- Reviewer note: the environment remains semantically faithful to the legacy two-mixed-Hives lane despite the dedicated Testcontainers implementation.

## Ported Test Classes

### `BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolutionJunit`


- Owning migration commit: `Add Hive environments`
- Current class added in same migration commit:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolutionJunit.java`
- Legacy class removed in same migration commit:
    - `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolution.java`
- Class-level environment requirement: none.
- Class-level tags: none.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `8`. Current methods: `8`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for all four methods. The JUnit port preserves the Hive-side view
  creation and Trino-side read/comment flows, and the restored compatibility helper again executes the legacy
  dual-Trino compatibility assertion.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for all five current methods against the single legacy source method. The old Tempto class expressed the storage-format matrix through five mutable-table requirements and one test method; the JUnit port expresses the same matrix as five methods that all delegate into `testSelectPartitionedTable`. The valid-partition assertion (`row(42, 2)`) and the tolerant handling of the invalid partition (`row(null, 1)` or thrown parse error) remain the same.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for both methods. The port preserves the same two ORC date-compatibility directions: Trino-written ORC remains readable by Hive/Trino with identical pre-Gregorian date values, and Hive-written ORC still reproduces the known Trino drift tracked in `trino/issues/26865`. The rewrite is mechanical only: environment helpers replace direct executors, and the Hive-written cleanup now drops through Hive instead of Trino.
- Reviewer note: manual source comparison confirmed that the 8 CSV wrapper tests still delegate to the same three private helper flows (`testInsertIntoCsvTable`, `testCreateCsvTableAs`, `testInsertIntoPartitionedCsvTable`, `testCreatePartitionedCsvTableAs`) and that the two multi-char-property tests preserve the direct Hive/Trino read-write behavior.
- Reviewer note: all 8 inherited Avro schema-evolution methods were manually re-read against the legacy base class in this semantic pass; the remaining subclass records below exist to document constructor and routing parity.

#### Methods

##### `testSelectTable`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolution.java` ->
  `testSelectTable`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolutionJunit.java` ->
  `BaseTestAvroSchemaEvolutionJunit.testSelectTable`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class does not declare a concrete environment requirement. Coverage is exercised through `TestAvroSchemaEvolutionOnSimpleTable` and `TestAvroSchemaEvolutionOnTableWithPartitioning`, both selected by `SuiteHiveStorageFormats` run 1 via `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`.
- Tag parity: Current tags: none. Routing comes from the concrete subclasses, which both add `StorageFormats` and are selected by `SuiteHiveStorageFormats`.
- Setup parity: Legacy setup shape: none. Current setup shape: none.
- Action parity: Legacy action shape: `SELECT`, `onTrino`, `executeQuery`, `format`. Current action shape: `SELECT`, `env.executeTrino`, `format`.
- Assertion parity: Legacy assertion helpers: `assertThat`, `containsExactlyInOrder`, `row`. Current assertion helpers: `assertThat`, `containsExactlyInOrder`, `row`.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy cleanup shape: none. Current cleanup shape: none.
- Any observed difference, however small: helper calls differ: legacy [onTrino, executeQuery, format] vs current [env.executeTrino, format]
- Known intentional difference: None.
- Reviewer note: Legacy flow summary -> helpers [onTrino, executeQuery, format], verbs [SELECT]. Current flow summary -> helpers [env.executeTrino, format], verbs [SELECT].
- Audit status: `verified`

- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `7`. Current methods: `7`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for all 7 methods. The port preserves the same schema-directory lifecycle checks, external-file preservation checks, and transactional drop/rollback coverage. The substantive implementation change is replacing injected `HdfsClient` / warehouse-path fields plus raw `onTrino().executeQuery(...)` calls with `HiveBasicEnvironment` accessors and `executeTrinoUpdate` / `executeTrinoInSession`, without changing the behaviors asserted.

#### Methods

##### `testCreateDropSchema`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestCreateDropSchema.java` ->
  `testCreateDropSchema`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestCreateDropSchema.java` ->
  `TestCreateDropSchema.testCreateDropSchema`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveBasicEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveBasic` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Both versions derive a random schema name, compute the warehouse-backed schema directory, create the schema, and assert the schema directory exists before table creation.
- Action parity: Both versions create a table inside the schema, attempt `DROP SCHEMA` while it is non-empty, then drop the table and schema in sequence.
- Assertion parity: Both versions assert the filesystem state before and after schema drop and assert that dropping a non-empty schema fails with the same non-empty-schema message text. The current port uses `assertThatThrownBy` instead of Tempto `assertQueryFailure`, but the asserted failure text is the same.
- Cleanup parity: Both versions drop the created table and then the schema, finally asserting that the schema directory is removed.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current code obtains `HdfsClient` and warehouse path from `HiveBasicEnvironment` instead of injected fields and uses `executeTrinoUpdate` rather than `onTrino().executeQuery(...)`; failure assertion API changed from Tempto query failure helpers to AssertJ throwable assertions.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Legacy flow summary -> helpers [randomNameSuffix, format, onTrino, executeQuery, s.test_drop], verbs [CREATE, DROP]. Current flow summary -> helpers [env.createHdfsClient, env.getWarehouseDirectory, randomNameSuffix, format, env.executeTrinoUpdate, s.test_drop], verbs [CREATE, DROP].
- Audit status: `verified`

- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveBasicEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveBasic` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Both versions create a default-location schema and capture the schema directory path before beginning transaction checks.
- Action parity: Both versions exercise two transactional paths: rollback of `DROP SCHEMA`, then commit of `DROP SCHEMA`, with a create/drop table sanity check between them.
- Assertion parity: Both versions assert that rollback preserves the schema directory and that commit removes it.
- Cleanup parity: Both versions leave the schema removed at the end of the committed path; the current port executes the multi-statement flows through `executeTrinoInSession`, which preserves the same transactional semantics as the legacy sequential `START TRANSACTION` / `ROLLBACK` / `COMMIT` calls.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current code uses `executeTrinoInSession` to bind transaction statements to one session, while the legacy code issued explicit transaction statements through Tempto `onTrino().executeQuery(...)`; this is a mechanical port change that preserves the same rollback/commit behavior under test.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`

Current flow summary -> helpers [env.createHdfsClient, env.getWarehouseDirectory, randomNameSuffix, format, env.executeTrinoUpdate, env.executeTrinoInSession, session.executeUpdate, s.test_table], verbs [CREATE, DROP].
- Audit status: `verified`

### `TestHdfsSyncPartitionMetadata`


- Owning migration commit: `Migrate TestHdfsSyncPartitionMetadata to JUnit`
- Current class added in same migration commit:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHdfsSyncPartitionMetadata.java`
- Legacy class removed in same migration commit:
    - `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHdfsSyncPartitionMetadata.java`
- Class-level environment requirement: `HiveBasicEnvironment`.
- Class-level tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `10`. Current methods: `10`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for all 10 methods. In legacy, 9 of the 10 methods were thin overrides that delegated to `BaseTestSyncPartitionMetadata`, while `testAddNonConventionalHivePartition` lived in the subclass. The JUnit port inlines the inherited base-class logic into one concrete class using `HiveBasicEnvironment` and explicit HDFS helpers. That is a structural rewrite, not a behavioral one: the same `sync_partition_metadata` modes, mixed-case path handling, URL-encoding checks, null-argument validation, and unconventional-partition behavior are still exercised.

### `TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest`


- Owning migration commit: `Migrate TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest to JUnit`
- Current class added in same migration commit:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest.java`

- Class-level tags: `HiveKerberos`.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `1`. Current methods: `1`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Reviewer note: The single method was manually re-read against the legacy class. The old TestNG version used `@BeforeMethodWithContext` / `@AfterMethodWithContext` to own the executor; the JUnit port creates and shuts down the executor inside the method, but still runs the same long CTAS, waits for ticket expiry, cancels the running query, and asserts the explicit-cancel message.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.

#### Methods

##### `kerberosTicketExpiryTest`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest.java` ->
  `kerberosTicketExpiryTest`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest.java` ->
  `TestHiveConnectorKerberosSmokeTest.kerberosTicketExpiryTest`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveKerberosEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveKerberos` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HiveKerberos`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Both versions disable writer scaling before launching the long-running CTAS. The current JUnit method also drops `hive.default.orders` first so reruns start clean.
- Action parity: Both versions submit `CREATE TABLE ... AS SELECT * FROM tpch.sf1000.orders` on a background thread, sleep for 60 seconds to force at least one Kerberos ticket expiry, locate the running query in `system.runtime.queries`, and kill it with the same explicit test message.
- Assertion parity: Legacy asserted `expected.getCause().hasMessageEndingWith("Message: explicitly cancelled for test without failure")`; current JUnit asserts the root cause message contains `explicitly cancelled for test without failure`. The cancellation intent and failure mode being checked are the same, but the current assertion is slightly looser.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy executor cleanup lived in TestNG hooks and left the created table behind if the query failed before completion; current JUnit always drops `hive.default.orders` and shuts down the executor in `finally`.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit localizes executor lifecycle to the test body, explicitly drops the target table before and after the run, and matches the cancellation message with `contains` instead of the legacy `endsWith` check.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Faithful port with stronger cleanup. The only semantic softening is `contains` vs `endsWith` on the cancellation message.
- Audit status: `verified`

### `TestHiveHiddenFiles`


- Owning migration commit: `Migrate TestHiveHiddenFiles to JUnit`

- Class-level environment requirement: `HiveBasicEnvironment`.
- Class-level tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `2`. Current methods: `2`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Reviewer note: Both methods were manually re-read against the legacy class. The legacy tests used a mutable loaded copy of `NATION_PARTITIONED_BY_BIGINT_REGIONKEY`; the JUnit port recreates an equivalent 15-row partitioned ORC table inline before each method, then drops it in `finally`. The required-session-property checks, schema-scoped override checks, and expected `p_regionkey` error text are otherwise preserved.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.
- Reviewer note: Manual source comparison completed for both methods. The port preserves the same hidden-file visibility scenarios: files renamed to `.` / `_` prefixes become invisible to both Trino and Hive, while files renamed to `f_...` remain visible. The changes are mechanical only: `HiveBasicEnvironment` now provides the HDFS client and table-path helper logic replaces the old `TableLocationUtils` utility.

### `TestHiveMaterializedView`


- Owning migration commit: `Migrate TestHiveMaterializedView to JUnit`
- Current class added in same migration commit:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java`
- Legacy class removed in same migration commit:
    - `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java`
- Class-level environment requirement: `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`.
- Class-level tags: `StorageFormats`.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `2`. Current methods: `2`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Reviewer note: Both methods were manually re-read against the legacy class. The shared transactional source-table setup is preserved. The non-partitioned materialized-view method remains faithful. The partitioned materialized-view method now documents a real Hive 3.1 parser limitation: the legacy stack accepted `PARTITIONED ON (x)` materialized views, while the current environment rejects that syntax and the JUnit port asserts the rejection.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`
- Method statuses present: `verified`, `intentional difference`.

#### Methods

##### `testMaterializedView`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java` ->
  `testMaterializedView`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java` ->
  `TestHiveMaterializedView.testMaterializedView`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveStorageFormats` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `StorageFormats`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Legacy and current classes both create the same transactional ORC source table with rows `('a'), ('a'), ('b')` in per-test setup before the materialized-view work begins.
- Action parity: Both versions create the non-partitioned materialized view over `SELECT x, count(*) c FROM test_materialized_view_table GROUP BY x`, then run the same metadata, read, filtered-read, and rejected-write checks.
- Assertion parity: Both versions assert that `SHOW TABLES` exposes the table and view, `SHOW COLUMNS` returns `c` and `x`, reads return `('a', 2)` and `('b', 1)`, the `WHERE x = 'a'` query returns `('a', 2)`, and writes fail with `Cannot write to Hive materialized view`.
- Cleanup parity: Both versions drop the source table per test; current JUnit additionally guards materialized-view drop in `finally` so cleanup still runs if creation/read assertions fail.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit wraps materialized-view cleanup more defensively, but the supported non-partitioned materialized-view behavior under test is unchanged.
- Known intentional difference: None.
- Reviewer note: Faithful port for the supported non-partitioned materialized-view case.
- Audit status: `verified`

##### `testPartitionedMaterializedView`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java` ->
  `testPartitionedMaterializedView`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveMaterializedView.java` ->
  `TestHiveMaterializedView.testPartitionedMaterializedView`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveStorageFormats` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `StorageFormats`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Both versions start from the same transactional ORC source table seeded in per-test setup.
- Action parity: Legacy method called the shared helper with `partitioned = true`, successfully created a partitioned materialized view, and then executed the same metadata/read/write assertions as the non-partitioned case. Current JUnit still builds the same `CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ... PARTITIONED ON (x)` statement but stops at creation because Hive 3.1 rejects the syntax.
- Assertion parity: Legacy asserted successful creation plus metadata/read/write behavior. Current JUnit asserts that Hive throws a parser error containing `mismatched input 'PARTITIONED' expecting AS`.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy helper dropped the created materialized view after the assertions. Current JUnit returns immediately after the parser failure, so only the source-table cleanup remains relevant.
- Any observed difference, however small: Partitioned materialized-view support was lost with the Hive 3.1 environment change; the current test records parser rejection rather than legacy successful behavior.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`
- Reviewer note: This is a substantive environment-driven drift, not just a helper rewrite. The port honestly records that current Hive 3.1 cannot perform the legacy partitioned materialized-view scenario.
- Audit status: `intentional difference`

The port preserves the same Trino-view and Hive-view `$properties` checks, including reading the backing Hive `SHOW TBLPROPERTIES` values and comparing them against Trino’s system table. - Class-level tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`.
- Method inventory complete: Yes. Legacy methods: `2`. Current methods: `2`.
- Legacy helper/resource dependencies accounted for: Legacy class source reviewed directly.
- Intentional differences summary: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Method statuses present: `verified`.

#### Methods

##### `testRequiresQueryPartitionFilter`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.java` ->
  `testRequiresQueryPartitionFilter`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.java` ->
  `TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.testRequiresQueryPartitionFilter`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveBasicEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveBasic` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Legacy method re-used a mutable loaded copy of `NATION_PARTITIONED_BY_BIGINT_REGIONKEY` under alias `test_table`; current JUnit method creates an equivalent ORC table with the same partition column `p_regionkey` and the same 15-row, 3-partition data set before enabling the session property.
- Action parity: Both versions set `hive.query_partition_filter_required = true`, run `SELECT COUNT(*)` without a partition predicate expecting failure, then run `SELECT COUNT(*) ... WHERE p_regionkey = 1` expecting `5`.
- Assertion parity: Both versions assert the same filter-required message for `p_regionkey` and the same successful filtered row count. The current port uses `assertThatThrownBy` instead of Tempto `assertQueryFailure`, but still checks the same message shape.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy relied on Tempto mutable-table cleanup; current JUnit method drops the inline-created table in `finally`.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit method synthesizes the partitioned fixture inline instead of using Tempto mutable-table loading, and it adds explicit `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` cleanup around the test-owned table.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Semantic intent is preserved. The only material implementation change is replacing Tempto fixture injection with an inline table builder that reproduces the same partition-count scenario.
- Audit status: `verified`

##### `testRequiresQueryPartitionFilterOnSpecificSchema`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.java` ->
  `testRequiresQueryPartitionFilterOnSpecificSchema`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.java` ->
  `TestHiveRequireQueryPartitionsFilter.testRequiresQueryPartitionFilterOnSpecificSchema`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveBasicEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveBasic` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Same as the legacy method, except the Tempto mutable-table fixture is recreated inline as a 15-row partitioned ORC table before the session properties are set.
- Action parity: Both versions set `hive.query_partition_filter_required = true`, set `hive.query_partition_filter_required_schemas` from the data provider value, then verify that the unfiltered `COUNT(*)` fails and the `p_regionkey = 1` query succeeds with `5` rows.
- Assertion parity: Failure-message and success-row-count checks are semantically the same; only the exception helper changed from Tempto `assertQueryFailure` to AssertJ `assertThatThrownBy`.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy cleanup was implicit through Tempto mutable-table state; current JUnit drops the inline-created table in `finally`.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit port creates and tears down the partitioned fixture explicitly instead of relying on Tempto mutable-table provisioning.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: The schema-scoped override behavior is preserved exactly; the only meaningful change is how the test table is provisioned and cleaned up.
- Audit status: `verified`

- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Legacy setup created `hive.default.test_sys_schema_disabled_table_in_default` in `@BeforeMethodWithContext`; current JUnit method performs the same `DROP IF EXISTS` + `CREATE TABLE` sequence inline before exercising the schema checks.
- Action parity: Both versions run the same `SHOW`, `SHOW TABLES`, `SHOW COLUMNS`, `DESCRIBE`, `SELECT`, and `information_schema.*` queries to prove that `sys` stays hidden from the Hive connector surface.
- Assertion parity: Both versions check the same presence/absence facts (`information_schema` and `default` visible, `sys` absent) and the same `Schema 'sys' does not exist` / table-missing failure text. The current port rewrites the first-column checks as helper methods and uses `assertThatThrownBy` for failing queries, but the asserted semantics are unchanged.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy cleanup dropped the helper table in `@AfterMethodWithContext`; current JUnit method drops it in `finally`.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit localizes the helper-table setup/cleanup inside the method and replaces Tempto `Condition<QueryResult>` helpers with explicit first-column assertion helpers.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Semantic coverage is preserved. The port’s main mechanical change is moving the shared helper-table lifecycle from TestNG hooks into the body of each JUnit method.
- Audit status: `verified`

- Legacy source method:

  `TestHiveSchema.testHiveInformationSchemaFilteredOut`

- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveBasicEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveBasic` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `HdfsNoImpersonation`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Same helper-table setup as the first method: legacy used `@BeforeMethodWithContext`, current JUnit inlines the `DROP IF EXISTS` + `CREATE TABLE` pair before checking `information_schema`.
- Action parity: Both versions enumerate the allowed `information_schema` tables, verify that Hive-only extras such as `column_privileges` stay hidden, and query `schemata`, `tables`, `columns`, and `table_privileges` to confirm Trino exposes its filtered `information_schema` view instead of Hive’s full one.
- Assertion parity: Table-name lists, column-name lists, and privilege visibility checks match the legacy method. The current port uses direct helper assertions and pre-materialized `QueryResult` variables, but it still checks the same allowed tables, same absence of `column_privileges`, and same failure for `SELECT * FROM hive.information_schema.column_privileges`.
- Cleanup parity: Legacy cleanup was the shared `@AfterMethodWithContext` table drop; current JUnit drops the helper table in `finally`.
- Any observed difference, however small: Current JUnit materializes intermediate `QueryResult` objects instead of chaining directly from `onTrino().executeQuery(...)`, and it replaces Tempto `Condition` helpers with explicit first-column assertion helpers; behavior checked stays the same.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Semantic intent is preserved. The JUnit port is more explicit about intermediate result materialization and helper-table lifecycle, but the visible `information_schema` contract being asserted is unchanged.
- Audit status: `verified`

The port preserves the exact DROP/CREATE/SELECT failure/DROP flow against a Hive table carrying the Delta Lake provider property. The only meaningful implementation difference is the assertion API: the current port checks the thrown stack trace chain for the same rejection text instead of asserting directly on the Tempto query-failure message wrapper.

#### Methods

##### `testReadDeltaLakeTable`

- Legacy source method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/main/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveDeltaLakeTable.java` ->
  `testReadDeltaLakeTable`
- Current target method:
  `testing/trino-product-tests/src/test/java/io/trino/tests/product/hive/TestHiveDeltaLakeTable.java` ->
  `TestHiveDeltaLakeTable.testReadDeltaLakeTable`
- Mapping type: `direct`
- Environment parity: Current class requires `HiveStorageFormatsEnvironment`. Routed by source review into `SuiteHiveStorageFormats` run 1.
- Tag parity: Current tags: `StorageFormats`. Tag routing matches the current suite selection.
- Setup parity: Both versions first drop any leftover `test_delta_lake_table`, then create a Hive table with `TBLPROPERTIES ('spark.sql.sources.provider'='DELTA')`.
- Action parity: Both versions issue a Trino `SELECT *` against the Hive connector view of that table and expect rejection because it is actually a Delta Lake table.
- Assertion parity: Both versions assert on the same rejection text, `Cannot query Delta Lake table 'default.test_delta_lake_table'`. The current port uses AssertJ `assertThatThrownBy(...).hasStackTraceContaining(...)` instead of Tempto `assertQueryFailure(...).hasMessageContaining(...)`, so it accepts the message anywhere in the throwable chain rather than only in the top-level message.
- Cleanup parity: Both versions drop the table after the assertion path completes.
- Any observed difference, however small: Legacy code did not wrap the cleanup in `finally`; current code does. That makes cleanup more reliable but does not change the behavior under test. The assertion helper now checks the throwable chain instead of only the top-level query failure message.
- Known intentional difference: `HDP to Hive 3.1 migration`,
  `Kerberos deployment cleanup without intended coverage change`
- Reviewer note: Legacy flow summary -> helpers [onHive, executeQuery, test_delta_lake_table, TBLPROPERTIES, onTrino], verbs [DROP, CREATE, SELECT]. Current flow summary -> helpers [env.executeHiveUpdate, TBLPROPERTIES, env.executeTrino, hasStackTraceContaining], verbs [DROP, CREATE, SELECT].
- Audit status: `verified`
